parameterGroups.label: Tuning (Musical)
parameterGroups.rationale: Octave and semitone offsets used when UseFreqRatio is Off (musical tuning mode)

parameters.name: Octave Transpose
parameters.description: Coarse pitch offset in octaves when using musical tuning mode

parameters.name: Semi Tones
parameters.description: Fine pitch offset in semitones when using musical tuning mode

parameterGroups.label: Tuning (Ratio)
parameterGroups.rationale: Coarse and fine frequency ratio controls used when UseFreqRatio is On (harmonic ratio mode)

parameters.name: Use Freq Ratio
parameters.description: Switches between musical (octave/semitone) and harmonic ratio tuning modes

parameters.name: Coarse Freq Ratio
parameters.description: Harmonic index as a frequency multiplier where 1 is the root frequency

parameters.name: Fine Freq Ratio
parameters.description: Fine frequency offset as a fractional multiplier in harmonic tuning mode

parameterGroups.label: Waveshaping
parameterGroups.rationale: Saturation control that adds harmonics to the pure sine wave

parameters.name: Saturation Amount
parameters.description: Amount of waveshaping saturation applied to the sine wave to add harmonics

parameterGroups.label: Output
parameterGroups.rationale: Base-class output controls shared by all SoundGenerators

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels.

parameterGroups.label: Voice Management
parameterGroups.rationale: Base-class voice management parameters shared by all SoundGenerators

parameters.name: Kill Fade Time
parameters.description: The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer

standaloneModChains.name: Pitch Modulation
standaloneModChains.note: Modulates implicit base pitch derived from MIDI note number + tuning parameters

gaps.question: What is the exact processing order in calculateBlock()? Specifically: where does pitch modulation apply relative to sine lookup, saturation, and gain?
gaps.context: Need to author accurate pseudo-code showing the full per-voice signal chain.

gaps.question: What waveshaping formula does the Saturator use? Is it applied per-sample after the sine lookup?
gaps.context: Need to describe what SaturationAmount actually does to the sine waveform.

gaps.question: How exactly is the frequency ratio calculated from CoarseFreqRatio and FineFreqRatio? The C++ in setInternalAttribute shows a non-trivial formula with different branches for positive/negative coarse values.
gaps.context: Users need to understand what values to set for specific harmonic relationships.

gaps.question: Does UseFreqRatio completely disable OctaveTranspose/SemiTones and vice versa? Are both sets of parameters always applied, or is it a strict either/or?
gaps.context: Need to describe the interaction between the two tuning modes clearly.

gaps.question: How is the mono sine signal converted to stereo? Is the left channel simply copied to right, or is Balance applied before the copy?
gaps.context: Need to show the mono-to-stereo step accurately in the signal path.

gaps.question: What size is the sine lookup table? Is interpolation linear or higher-order?
gaps.context: Relevant for describing the quality/performance trade-off of this lightweight module.

notes: SineSynth is explicitly described as lightweight in the C++ doc comment - recommended for simple uses, not additive synthesis (per-instance pitch modulation overhead). The module has two mutually exclusive tuning modes controlled by UseFreqRatio toggle. The saturation feature adds harmonics via waveshaping, turning the pure sine into a richer tone. Base-class parameters (Gain, Balance, VoiceLimit, KillFadeTime) are inherited from ModulatorSynth.
